rulings.Case Title: 222 KEARNY PROPERTY, L.P. VS. ADAMS NYE BECHT, LLP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Adams Nye Becht, Llp.'S Motion For Withdrawal Of Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 09, 2020 line 1. Adams Nye Becht, Llp.'S Motion For Withdrawal Of Attorney Of Record READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is GRANTED. Counsel to prepare order. Defendant to obtain new counsel no later than 30 days after the bankruptcy stay is lifted. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ VS. LEANA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 07, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANTs LEANA PEREZ and FRANCISCO ARMAS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to the Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The complaint fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. It is unclear whether Plaintiffs nuisance claims originate from a violation of the house rules or from Defendants behavior generally. Plaintiffs should file an amended complaint clearly focused on the nuisance claims unrelated to a breach of the house rules only, as the tenancy allegations are the subject of another action. = (501/CFH)
